// SLIDE 01 — HOOK

WHAT COWORK IS FOR.

By the end of this chapter you will be able to decide whether a task belongs in Cowork, Claude chat, Claude Code, or hum

NARRATION

By the end of this chapter you will be able to decide whether a task belongs in Cowork, Claude chat, Claude Code, or human-only judgment , before you start working.

// SLIDE 02 — FOUR TASKS WALK INTO YOUR INBO

FOUR TASKS WALK INTO YOUR INBOX.

First:→Second:→Third:→Fourth:
NARRATION

It is Tuesday morning. You have four things on your list. First: A colleague sent you a draft memo and asked you to make the tone crisper. One file, one pass, done.

// SLIDE 03 — THE FOUR ROUTES

THE FOUR ROUTES.

Chat→Cowork→Claude Code→Human-only
NARRATION

The book's central routing question: Is this task mainly conversation or thinking? Does it require files, folders, and saved artifacts? Does it require codebase action or command execution? Is it sensitive, regulated, irreversible, or judgment-heavy in a way that requires a human to stay in the chair? Chat is for thinking, drafting, explaining, critiquing, brainstorming, and short source-grounded tasks where you stay close to the output and no files are needed beyond what you paste directly. The memo rewrite is chat.

// SLIDE 04 — WHAT MAKES A TASK COWORK-SHAPE

WHAT MAKES A TASK COWORK-SHAPED.

·Multiple source files that all need to be read
·An output that must be saved rather than just displayed
·More than one or two steps in sequence
·A result you can define in advance and verify afterward
·Source materials in a bounded, accessible folder
NARRATION

Cowork fits tasks with the following signals (Anthropic, "Get started with Claude Cowork"): Multiple source files that all need to be read An output that must be saved rather than just displayed More than one or two steps in sequence A result you can define in advance and verify afterward Source materials in a bounded, accessible folder

// SLIDE 05 — THE ROUTING MATRIX

THE ROUTING MATRIX.

Need ideas, draft text, or critique — Chat
Need files assembled into an artifact — Cowork
Need code changes, tests, or a diff — Claude Code
Need final judgment on a person's situation — Human-only
NARRATION

This matrix is a starting point, not a formula. Parasuraman, Sheridan, and Wickens (2000) developed a model for types and levels of human interaction with automation that makes a similar point: the right level of automation for a task depends on context, reliability, consequences, and the human's ability to detect errors. Higher automation is not always better automation.

// SLIDE 06 — THE IRONIES OF ROUTING WRONG

THE IRONIES OF ROUTING WRONG.

Bainbridge's classic 1983 paper "Ironies of Automation" identified a trap that applies directly to Cowork routing: the m

The Ironies of Routing Wrong — Routing a task to chat when it should be Cowork wastes your time on manual assembly. Routing a task to Cowork when it sh
NARRATION

Bainbridge's classic 1983 paper "Ironies of Automation" identified a trap that applies directly to Cowork routing: the more you delegate to automation, the more critical your own skill and attention becomes when the automation fails or reaches its limits. If you route a task to Cowork when it is not ready for Cowork , vague sources, ambiguous goal, no verification path , you do not get a free result. You get a plausible-looking artifact that may be wrong in ways that are hard to catch.

// SLIDE 07 — THINKING THROUGH THE FOUR CASE

THINKING THROUGH THE FOUR CASES.

Memo rewrite:→Operations packet:→Website script error:→Student accommodation records:
NARRATION

Memo rewrite: Chat. Paste the memo, describe the tone, review the reply. If the memo is one document and the task is one pass, there is no workspace to prepare, no artifact to save, and no multi-step plan to review. Cowork would be setup without payoff. Operations packet: Cowork. The task has three source files in separate locations, a structured output format, and a recurring weekly shape that rewards a saved project.

// SLIDE 08 — WHEN CHAT IS ENOUGH — AND THAT

WHEN CHAT IS ENOUGH — AND THAT IS GOOD

·Drafting short documents from materials you paste directly
·Brainstorming, critiquing, and explaining
·Summarizing a single document you can share
·Answering questions with reasoning or analysis
·Revising text with feedback
NARRATION

A common mistake is treating Cowork as the more advanced, therefore better, surface. It is not. It is a different surface for a different task shape. Chat is excellent for: Drafting short documents from materials you paste directly Brainstorming, critiquing, and explaining Summarizing a single document you can share Answering questions with reasoning or analysis Revising text with feedback

// SLIDE 09 — HUMAN-ONLY IS NOT FAILURE

HUMAN-ONLY IS NOT FAILURE.

Decisions with real consequences for real peoplepersonnel, accommodations, benefits, legal status
Regulated informationhealth records, student records, financial filings, legal documents
Irreversible actionsdeleting files permanently, sending external communications, making commitments on behalf of others
Ethically ambiguous judgmentsituations where values, context, and relationships matter more than pattern matching
NARRATION

The fourth route , human-only , deserves its own defense. In workplaces under pressure to use AI everywhere, it is easy to read "human-only" as "stuck in the past" or "not using the tool well." That reading is wrong. Human-only is the appropriate designation for: Decisions with real consequences for real people: personnel, accommodations, benefits, legal status Regulated information: health records, student records, financial filings, legal documents Irreversible actions: deleting files permanently, sending external communications, making commitments on behalf of others Ethically ambiguous judgment: situations where values, context,

// SLIDE 10 — THE HUMAN GATE IN THIS CHAPTER

THE HUMAN GATE IN THIS CHAPTER.

·Is this task mainly conversation, or does it require files and a saved artifact?
·Does it require codebase action?
·Does it involve sensitive, regulated, or irreversible consequences?
Is there a clear verification pathcould I check whether the output is right?
NARRATION

The routing decision is itself a human judgment. Cowork cannot decide whether a task should be in Cowork. You can. Before opening any session, ask: Is this task mainly conversation, or does it require files and a saved artifact? Does it require codebase action? Does it involve sensitive, regulated, or irreversible consequences? Is there a clear verification path , could I check whether the output is right?

// SLIDE 11 — COMMON MISTAKES

COMMON MISTAKES

"Cowork is always better than chat."→"The most powerful surface is the right surface."→"A repetitive task is automatically safe to delegate."→"Code work is just another document task."→"Human-only is a failure to use AI."
NARRATION

"Cowork is always better than chat." Cowork has setup cost , workspace, brief, plan, review. For a simple task, that cost exceeds the benefit. "The most powerful surface is the right surface." Routing by power rather than fit is how work gets slower, not faster.

// SLIDE 12 — THESIS

THE CORE CLAIM.

"Cowork is always better than chat." Cowork has setup cost — workspace, brief, plan, review. For a simple task, that cos

"The most powerful surface is the right surface." Routing by power rather than fit is how work gets slower, not faster.

NARRATION

"Cowork is always better than chat." Cowork has setup cost , workspace, brief, plan, review. For a simple task, that cost exceeds the benefit. "The most powerful surface is the right surface." Routing by power rather than fit is how work gets slower, not faster.
